title: FRMI Earnings Review
markdown:
I have strong material. Let me gather prior-quarter comparison and more detail on capacity, tenants, and guidance.
Let me get analyst commentary and the precise stock reaction to the print.
# FRMI — Latest Quarter Review

**Q1 FY2026 (quarter ended March 31, 2026) | Reported May 14, 2026 | Shares spiked ~8.5% pre-market to ~$6.51 on a narrower-than-feared loss, but gave back gains and were later downgraded by Evercore [3,4,6]**

## The quarter in brief

Fermi Inc. (NASDAQ/LSE: FRMI), the pre-revenue developer of the "Project Matador" private AI-power campus in the Texas Panhandle, reported a Q1 FY2026 net loss of **$189 million** (about $188.7M), or **$0.30 per diluted share**, with management noting roughly **70% of the loss was non-cash** — driven primarily by share-based compensation tied to its broad employee equity program [1,3,5]. The print was actually *better* than feared: the $0.30 loss came in ahead of the ~$0.50 per-share loss consensus polled by Fiscal AI, which helped shares pop ~8.5% pre-market to about $6.51 from $6 before the rally faded [4,6]. The loss also absorbed roughly **$134M of non-cash share-based compensation** and a **$25M loss on debt extinguishment** related to the Macquarie Term Loan [3]. As a pre-revenue company, Fermi again generated **$0 of revenue** against a small (~$600k) modeled figure [4].

For this asset-heavy, pre-revenue profile, the balance sheet and cash flow matter more than the income statement. Fermi ended the quarter with **$243 million in total cash and restricted cash**, down from **$409 million at December 31, 2025** — a meaningful drawdown driven by capex, not operating burn [3,12]. Notably, **cash used in operating activities was only ~$7 million**, aided by ~$22M of favorable net working capital [2]. The real cash consumption was capital deployment: Fermi invested **$441 million in property, plant and equipment** during the quarter, lifting cumulative investment in Project Matador to **over $1.4 billion** [2,3].

The single most important development was less a number than a posture shift: management unveiled **"Fermi 2.0,"** reframing the company from founder-led startup to "institutional" operator following the April resignations of co-founder/CEO Toby Neugebauer and CFO Miles Everson, and set a **90-day plan** centered on landing a *binding* tenant agreement [1,9,10]. After the September 2025 first-tenant LOI's $150M construction-funding agreement was terminated in December 2025, Fermi still has **no executed definitive lease** — making tenant conversion the gating catalyst for project financing and construction restart [7,8].

## Key metrics

| Metric | Q1 FY2026 (ended 3/31/26) | Prior period | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Revenue | $0 (pre-revenue) | $0 (pre-revenue) | [4,6] |
| Net loss | $189M (~$188.7M) | FY2025: $486M (full year) | [1,3,12] |
| EPS (diluted) | $(0.30) | — | [3] |
| % of loss non-cash | ~70% | FY2025: ~91% (~$445M) | [3,12] |
| Non-cash share-based comp | ~$134M | FY2025: $133M | [3,12] |
| Loss on debt extinguishment | $25M (Macquarie Term Loan) | — | [3] |
| Cash used in operating activities | ~$7M | FY2025: $34M (full year) | [2,12] |
| Capex (PP&E) | $441M | FY2025: ~$570M (full year) | [2,12] |
| Cumulative Project Matador investment | >$1.4B | — | [1,3] |
| Total cash & restricted cash | $243M | $409M (12/31/25); $183M (9/30/25) | [3,12,13] |
| New equipment financing (quarter) | $785M (incl. $500M MUFG) | — | [2] |
| Additional financing (Yorkville, corporate) | $156M | — | [2] |
| Generation capacity secured | ~2 GW | ~2.2 GW secured/under LOI (Q3'25) | [8,13] |
| Permitted / planned capacity | ~6 GW air permit; ~11 GW planned | ~6 GW air permit (Q4'25) | [4,12] |

*Note on debt: press-release coverage cited total debt of roughly **$131M**, while post-print analyst coverage referenced ~**$421M** of debt on the balance sheet; the figures differ by source and likely reflect different treatment of equipment-financing facilities, so I flag rather than reconcile them [3,6].*

## Guidance & management commentary

Management did not issue traditional financial guidance (it remains pre-revenue) but laid out a concrete **90-day execution plan** under Chairman Marius Haas: (1) **secure a binding tenant agreement**, (2) diligently **manage working capital and liquidity**, (3) **hire a permanent CEO**, (4) **explore strategic partnerships** to accelerate power/data-center deployment, and (5) **deliver power at the project site** [1,2].

On the commercial pipeline, management said the original first-tenant LOI "remains in place" as a commercial framework and that the pipeline **expanded after exclusivity expired in December**, with multiple prospective counterparties — a mix of hyperscale cloud operators, AI-infrastructure firms, chip manufacturers and enterprise compute users — at various stages including draft lease documentation, term-sheet evaluation, and confirmatory site diligence, drawn by the ~6 GW air permit and construction readiness [7]. On cash burn, Interim CFO Rob Masson said equipment financing covers most turbine/electrical equipment spend and that capital deployment will be **matched to new tenant agreements** [2]. Interim Co-CEO Anna Bofa expressed confidence in landing a tenant within 90 days, citing resolution of prior customer concerns and streamlined site processes [2].

Importantly, on timelines, the company had previously targeted **~1.1 GW online by end-2026** but disclosed in a recent SEC filing that it **no longer expects to meet that target**; on the prior call management cited tenant-designed cooling systems as a bigger-than-expected bottleneck, and said construction will not advance until a definitive tenant agreement and project financing are in place [9].

## Surprises & risks

**Surprises:**
- **Loss "beat":** the $0.30 loss was narrower than the ~$0.50 consensus, and the stock initially rallied despite zero revenue [4,6].
- **Financing momentum:** Fermi added **$785M of new equipment-financing facilities (incl. $500M from MUFG)** plus **$156M from Yorkville** for general corporate purposes — addressing the funding overhang for equipment, if not for construction broadly [2].
- **Leadership overhaul:** the abrupt April departures of the co-founder/CEO and CFO, plus a governance fight (the board reportedly raised a bylaw threshold to 70% while Neugebauer pushed a competing slate), are a material narrative reset for a still-young public company [9,10].

**Key risks:**
1. **No binding lease = no project finance:** without a definitive tenant, large-scale construction and non-recourse project financing are stalled; the 90-day target is aggressive given a prior LOI already collapsed [7,8].
2. **Schedule slippage:** the ~1.1 GW year-end-2026 first-power target has been pulled, and third-party analysis suggested first buildings may not energize until ~mid-2027 [9].
3. **Liquidity/governance:** cash fell to $243M with continued heavy capex; management has previously warned that if financing isn't secured on favorable terms it could delay investments or surrender collateral — layered on top of leadership and boardroom instability [3,12].

## What to watch next quarter

- **Binding tenant agreement** — did Fermi convert any of its pipeline into an executed definitive lease within the stated 90-day window? [1,2,7]
- **Permanent CEO hire** and resolution (or escalation) of the Neugebauer-vs-board governance contest [1,9,10].
- **Cash trajectory** — sequential cash/restricted-cash balance vs. the $243M exit level, and whether operating burn stays minimal while capex is throttled to tenant progress [2,3].
- **Project financing close** following any lease, and any change to the (now-withdrawn) first-power timeline [9].
- **Capacity milestones** — progress beyond ~2 GW secured generation, Siemens SGT-800 turbine deliveries, and on-site "first power" [8,9].
- **Strategic partnership / M&A** outcomes from the Fermi 2.0 review [1].

## Sources

1. PR Newswire / Fermi Inc. — "Fermi Inc. Outlines Fermi 2.0 Strategic Evolution and Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results" (press release), May 2026.
2. Yahoo Finance / GuruFocus — "Fermi Inc (FRMI) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Highlights / Transcript," May 14, 2026.
3. Investing.com — "Fermi America reports $189M Q1 loss, secures $785M financing," May 2026.
4. Investing.com — "Earnings call transcript: Fermi America Q1 2026 reveals $189M loss," May 2026.
5. Yahoo Finance — "Fermi Inc. (FRMI) Q1 FY2026 earnings call transcript," May 2026.
6. Stocktwits — "FRMI Stock... Evercore Acknowledges Reset At Fermi But Awaits Proof To Turn Bullish," May 2026.
7. SEC Form 8-K (FY2026), Fermi Inc. shareholder letter — tenant LOI / commercial pipeline disclosure.
8. Investing.com / SEC filings — "Fermi Inc. tenant ends $150 million construction funding agreement at Project Matador," December 12, 2025.
9. Axios — "Trump-branded AI data center megaproject stalls, CEO departs," April 19, 2026.
10. ANS Nuclear Newswire — "CEO and CFO of Fermi America step down," April 22, 2026.
11. The Register — "Trump-branded datacenter fails to make itself great, again," April 20, 2026.
12. AOL / Yahoo Finance / Insider Monkey — Fermi (FRMI) Q4 & FY2025 earnings call coverage (cash $409M; FY2025 net loss $486M), March 30–31, 2026.
13. SEC Form 8-K (FY2025), Fermi Inc. Q3 2025 shareholder letter (cash $183M; capacity targets), Nov 2025.

*Not investment advice.*
